mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or median 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumption capacity, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, generating sustained upward pressure on equity markets over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of stable monetary policy reflects weakening manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, leading to negative equity returns as corporate profit margins contract.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS changes by more than 0.25% in the preceding quarter, or Rank IC turns positive during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which systematically supports positive equity market performance over the following month.
failure_condition: Median 20-day forward returns following positive payroll revisions are negative, or the correlation between payroll growth and equity returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2% over 60 days following CPI spikes above the threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weakening final demand, causing equity markets to price in impending corporate earnings declines over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable inflation signals healthy capacity expansion and robust corporate output, driving positive equity returns as profit margins improve during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 65% of triggered instances during recovery regimes, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
